reason: Four NOR gates: NOR(A,A)=A', NOR(B,B)=B', NOR(A',B')=A.B, NOR(AB,AB)=(AB)' -> NAND.
Y = A'.(A+B) and Z = Y + (A+B); with A=B=1: A'=0 so Y=0, and A+B=1 so Z=1.
Circuit switching establishes a dedicated end-to-end channel for the call duration; packet switching shares links.
Bus topology lets devices tap the shared trunk anywhere with no central hub or closed loop constraint, so wiring is most flexible; question is loosely worded.
Modem is for WAN/telephone-line access, not a LAN component; cable, printer and computer all are.
A proxy is the intermediary machine holding external/Internet access on behalf of internal clients.
A firewall filters/blocks unauthorized external access; gateway and router forward traffic, virus checker targets malware.
Granting full access rights to all users lets anyone alter data, breaching integrity; the others are physical protections.
IPv4 total-length field is 16 bits, so max datagram size = 2^16 - 1 = 65535 bytes.
unanswerable - options are garbled/truncated dotted-quads; 10 equal subnets of a /24 needs 255.255.255.240 (/28), which no option states. No source key either.
Class C uses 24 network bits + 8 host bits.
IANA = Internet Assigned Numbers Authority.
Two IP versions are in use/deployed: IPv4 and IPv6.
TCP/IP host-to-host (transport) layer maps to OSI layer 4.
ECB is a symmetric block-cipher mode of operation; RSA and Diffie-Hellman are public-key.
A router interconnects two (or more) distinct networks at layer 3.
That is the standard definition of Message Oriented Middleware - asynchronous message exchange via queues.
127.0.0.1 is the canonical loopback host address (127.0.0.0 is the network address of the loopback block).
Ping sends ICMP echo request/reply messages.
POP and FTP are both application-layer; PPP is data-link. Option D is the combined choice, though its text is garbled ('a and d' should read 'a and c').
